title: ranunculus acris subsp. friesianus
intro: Ranunculus acris subsp. friesianus, also known as the Meadow Buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus acris plant, belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus acris subsp. friesianus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
